---
title: Testing
description: End-to-end test pattern used by every actpkg component — just + hurl + a live act server.
---

Every published `actpkg` component tests its tools end-to-end with the same pattern: boot the component under `act run --http`, then exercise every tool with [hurl](https://hurl.dev/). The fixture is a three-line `justfile` recipe; no per-component test harness.

## The recipe

```makefile
port := `npx get-port-cli`
addr := "[::1]:" + port
baseurl := "http://" + addr

test:
    #!/usr/bin/env bash
    set -euo pipefail
    act run {{wasm}} --http --listen "{{addr}}" &
    trap "kill $!" EXIT
    npx wait-on -t 180s {{baseurl}}/info
    npx @orangeopensource/hurl --test --variable "baseurl={{baseurl}}" e2e/*.hurl
```

Key points:

- `get-port-cli` avoids collisions when CI runs multiple components in parallel.
- `wait-on` polls `/info` (a no-instantiation endpoint) until the server is ready.
- `hurl` reads plain-text `.hurl` files — readable both by humans and as assertions.

## A `hurl` test

```hurl
# e2e/reverse.hurl
POST {{baseurl}}/tools/reverse
{"arguments": {"text": "hello"}}
HTTP 200
[Asserts]
jsonpath "$.content[0].data" == "olleh"
```

The ACT-HTTP wire format is documented in [ACT-HTTP](https://github.com/actcore/act-spec/blob/main/spec/ACT-HTTP.md). For quick tool calls during iteration, `act call` is usually faster:

```bash
act call <wasm> reverse --args '{"text":"hello"}'
```

## CI

The Rust and Python templates ship a `.github/workflows/ci.yml` that runs `just build` → `just test` → `just publish` (on `main`). On PRs only build+test run; publish only fires on `main` push.

## Capability flags in tests

If your component declares `wasi:filesystem` or `wasi:http`, the `test` recipe also needs runtime policy flags. SQLite's test recipe, for example:

```makefile
DB_DIR=$(mktemp -d)
act run {{wasm}} --http --listen "{{addr}}" \
  --fs-policy allowlist --fs-allow /dev/urandom --fs-allow "$DB_DIR" &
```

Ancestor directories on the way to an allowed path are implicitly traversable — you don't need to list each parent explicitly.
